Pragmatic Self contradiction is a kind of phrase which perspectivally contradicts itself for practical reasons<ref name=":0">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=udlkps-81JM</ref><ref name=":1">https://www.meaningcrisis.co/ep-40-awakening-from-the-meaning-crisis-wisdom-and-rationality/</ref><ref name=":2">[[Reacting to the Meaning Crisis]] episode 40</ref><ref name=":3">https://www.amazon.ca/dp/080148278X?tag=morzaram05-20&geniuslink=true</ref>

Uncontroversial example: "I am asleep" because you are saying it from a perspective of being awake<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":3" />

Controversial example: "I am wise" it is being said from a perspective of not being wise<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":3" />

Here is another source to use to enhance the article.<ref>[https://www.degruyter.com/document/doi/10.1515/SATS.2008.91/html?lang=en#:~:text=The%20'principle%20of%20pragmatic%20self,to%20be%20pragmatically%20self%2Dcontradictory. https://www.degruyter.com/document/doi/10.1515/SATS.2008.91/html?lang=en#:~:text=The%20'principle%20of%20pragmatic%20self,to%20be%20pragmatically%20self%2Dcontradictory.]</ref>

== References ==

[[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Pragmatic Self Contradiction]][[Category:Linguistics]]
[[Category:Pages]]
